---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Worker** — a `ddx work` / `ddx try` process scoped to a specific project
  root. Do not call every `ddx work` process a "DDx worker" when multiple
  projects are visible. If the user says "DDx worker" without a qualifier,
  assume they mean the worker for the current/requested project. Say "worker
  for `<project>`" or "DDx-powered worker"; reserve "DDx repo worker" for work
  whose project root is the DDx repository.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

data.output:
# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; Fizeau owns concrete
routing, provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching, catalog
lookups, and transcript/session rendering.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--watch` — keep running after the current ready queue drains; wait for newly-ready beads.
- `--idle-interval <dur>` — sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode (default 30s).
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the service's reported
  power bands.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` / `--profile <name>` — passthrough constraints only. DDx sends them
  unchanged and does not route on them.

## Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis

The worker's pre-claim intake hook evaluates a `readiness_checks` payload before
claiming a bead. The canonical verdict forms are:

- JSON bool `true` -> `pass`
- JSON bool `false` -> `fail`
- JSON strings are passed through after trimming
- `null` or absent -> empty

`ClassifyReadinessWithMode`
(`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:56-115`) is the mapping layer
between readiness classifications and worker behavior:

- `system_unready` / `intake_error` are hard errors from the hook, but the
  worker fail-opens and skips the claim instead of parking the bead.
- `needs_refine` is warn-only in warn mode and becomes operator-attention /
  park in block mode.
- `operator_required` parks the bead.
- `needs_split` parks the bead for decomposition.

When the worker appears to idle on a full queue, inspect `loop.idle` events
first. That event stream is the diagnostic surface for a silent stall. Repeated
identical blocker details are escalated after
`preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold` cycles (`ddx-df77e668`), which should convert
the stall into a `loop.operator_attention` signal instead of letting the worker
spin forever. The main blocker codes to distinguish are
`preclaim_systemic` and `preclaim_tracker_contention`.

If the queue is still making claims but the rate is suspiciously low, use the
claim-success-rate warning knobs `--claim-rate-window` and
`--claim-rate-threshold` to surface that condition.

## Are workers running for this project?

When someone asks "is the worker still working beads?" or "how is the queue
progressing?", do not answer with a global `ps aux | grep -E 'ddx work|ddx try'`
scan. That returns workers from every checkout on the host and falsely
signals progress on whichever project happens to be asked about.

```bash
ddx work status                # live workers scoped to this project
ddx work status --json         # machine-readable
ddx work status --all-projects # explicit cross-project escape hatch
```

`ddx work status` defaults to the current project root (CLI flag → env
`DDX_PROJECT_ROOT` → CWD git root). It reports pid, age, command line,
project root, and — when inferable from argv or an isolated execute-bead
worktree — the active bead ID. Always name the project root the answer
applies to; never substitute a worker from a different repository as
evidence that this project is progressing.

When multiple repositories have workers, report them project-first:

```text
<project-root>
  pid=<pid> bead=<bead-id> phase=<phase>
```

Use "worker for `<project-root>`" for project-scoped status. Use
"DDx-powered worker" for a generic `ddx work` process. Use "DDx repo worker"
only when the project root is the DDx repository. If the user says "DDx
worker" without a qualifier, assume the current/requested project and do not
surface workers from other projects.

`--all-projects` is the only sanctioned way to ask "what ddx workers
exist anywhere on this host?" — use it only when the question is
explicitly cross-project.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Starting and monitoring workers in the background

When the user asks you to **start workers and watch progress**, do
not invent your own `tail | grep` pipeline — the obvious shapes have
all bitten in the field. Use these vetted patterns.

### One worker, foreground drain

If the user just wants to drain once and read the result at the end,
run `ddx work` directly and let it print. Don't wrap with `head`,
`tail`, or `tee` — pipes to those tools buffer stdout for the entire
run, so the user sees nothing during execution and everything at the
end.

### One or more workers, watch live

```bash
# Start each worker, one log per worker:
ddx work > /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>&1 &   # repeat for worker2, etc.

# Stream with per-worker prefixes (drops the `==>` separator noise
# that `tail -F` injects when switching files):
(tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w1: "$0; fflush()}' &
 tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker2.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w2: "$0; fflush()}' &
 wait) | grep -E --line-buffered \
  "^w[0-9]+: (▶|✗|✓|→ |worker exited|attempts:|failed:|landing worktree has|lock_contention|panic|FATAL|excluded|caller_excluded|ladder exhausted|escalation|powerClass=|do route|i/o timeout|connection refused|provider error)"
```

Why those specific tokens — together they cover **every actionable
event** in a drain:

- `▶ <bead>` — a new bead was claimed. `✗` and `✓` close it.
- `→` — outcome arrow, also follows a bead-state transition.
- `worker exited` / `attempts:` / `failed:` — end-of-drain summary.
- `landing worktree has` — pre-claim hook hit a stale index; queue
  is jammed if recovery doesn't kick in.
- `lock_contention`, `panic`, `FATAL` — infrastructure problems.
- `excluded`, `caller_excluded`, `ladder exhausted`,
  `escalation`, `powerClass=` — the escalation path actually
  firing; absence on a failed bead means the loop is silently
  no-op'ing instead of climbing the ladder.
- `do route` — the implementation-phase routing decision, surfaces
  the provider/model that's about to be hit. Without this in the
  alternation, the monitor goes quiet during the most interesting
  window (provider call) and only resurfaces on success/failure.
- `i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, `provider error` — transport
  failures. Two in a row to the same `(provider, model)` is the
  signature of routing that isn't honoring exclusions.

### What NOT to do

- **Don't wrap `ddx work` in `| tail -N`** to "see the last N
  lines". The pipe buffers until the producer exits, so you see
  nothing for the whole run. Redirect to a file and tail the file
  separately.
- **Don't grep `^==>` into the alternation.** Without the awk
  prefix, `tail -F file1 file2` emits `==> filename <==` whenever
  the active file switches — every switch becomes a noise event in
  a Monitor pipeline. Strip those, or prefix lines with `awk`.
- **Don't tail without `--line-buffered`/`fflush()`.** Pipe
  buffering means events arrive in 4KB chunks (often minutes apart)
  instead of as they happen. The `awk` form above flushes per line.
- **Don't share one log file across workers.** Interleaved output
  destroys attributability and any chance of debugging which worker
  hit what.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

During a live `ddx work` / `ddx try` attempt, the current bead's lifecycle is
orchestrator-owned. The worker executing that bead must not run
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`,
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the
current bead. The Step 0 split-first path still allows child-bead
`ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and the parent-note update
`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:93:{"id":"corpus-612aeaa9052a","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard","description":"Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.

Context:
- A real PTY session against installed Gemini CLI 0.38.2 showed /stats route-target only reports route-target/session usage after an API call. It did not show quota remaining/limit/reset for the active concrete route-target.
- The same PTY session showed /route-target manage is the quota surface. Observed output included: Flash 4% used, reset 9:13 PM (23h 46m); Flash Lite 0% used, reset 9:27 PM (24h); Pro 100% used.
- Upstream Gemini CLI docs say /stats route-target shows token counts and quota information, but the installed CLI renders quota in the route-target management dialog. Treat both /stats route-target and /route-target manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.
- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by route-target/tier, with freshness and source recorded.

In-scope files:
- internal/runneres/gemini/ or internal/runneres/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.
- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.
- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.
- docs/helix/02-design/primary-runner-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/runner-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.

Out-of-scope:
- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.
- Do not promote Gemini to primary/auto-routing unless routing consumes the parsed quota cache and blocks exhausted tiers.
- Do not change Codex or Claude quota behavior except shared helper refactors required by the Gemini parser.","acceptance":"1. Add a Gemini PTY quota probe or cassette-backed parser that can parse /route-target manage output containing tier/route-target usage percentages and reset times, including exhausted tiers such as Pro 100% used.
2. Persist parsed Gemini quota evidence in a durable cache with source, captured_at, freshness, per-tier/route-target used percent or remaining/limit, and reset time.
3. ListRunneres reports Gemini quota only from parsed quota evidence, never from auth/account freshness alone. Missing or stale Gemini quota evidence is not ok.
4. Automatic routing does not select Gemini when quota evidence is missing/stale or the selected tier/route-target is exhausted; if promotion is enabled, it is gated by fresh parsed quota evidence.
5. Add tests covering the observed PTY output: Flash 4% used with reset, Flash Lite 0% used with reset, and Pro 100% used/exhausted.
6. go test ./... passes.","labels":["area:runner","area:gemini","kind:feature"],"bead":{"id":"agent-37659612","title":"Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:166:{"id":"corpus-b7113543d6ca","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","description":"## Motivation

DDx consumer goal G3: "the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**." Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. When two upstream-services serve the same route-target with different prices, the engine picks by name or score, not cost.

## Scope

### Data route-target

Add to `internal/routing.Upstream-serviceEntry`:

- `CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` — nil/zero if unknown.
- `CostSource string` — `"catalog" | "subscription" | "unknown" | "user-config"`. Surfaces in decision trace so operators know where the number came from.

Populate at `buildRoutingInputs` time:

- **Endpoint upstream-services (openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, minimax, qwen, zai, openai):** look up in the existing route-target catalog (`internal/route-targetcatalog`) by resolved route-target ID. When the catalog has a price, use it. When absent, leave zero + `CostSource: "unknown"`.
- **Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini):** compute an **effective** cost as a function of quota utilization:
  - At 0-70% quota used: effective cost = 0 (fixed-cost subscription, near-zero marginal).
  - At 70-90%: effective cost = low (encourage spreading load).
  - At 90-100%: effective cost = high (near-quota demotion).
  - Exact numbers in the bead acceptance; tunable via `ServiceOptions`.
  - `CostSource: "subscription"`.
- **`agent` runner (local via endpoints):** cost = electricity estimate from config (off by default — `CostSource: "unknown"` when not configured). Operators who want to route-target local cost set `ServiceOptions.LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens`.

### Scoring changes

`internal/routing/score.go`: add cost as a tiebreak dimension **after** eligibility, quota OK, and base score. Order becomes:
1. Eligible
2. Quota OK / healthy
3. Base score (profile policy, cooldowns, perf bias) — unchanged
4. **Cost (lower is better)** — new
5. Locality (local cost-class preferred)
6. Name

For unknown-cost upstream-services (`CostSource: "unknown"`): treat as **neutral** in the cost tiebreak — neither preferred nor demoted, so they don't starve known-cost upstream-services but aren't penalized into last place.

### Unknown-cost policy

A documented rule: when all viable candidates have `CostSource: "unknown"`, the cost tiebreak is skipped and locality/name decide. Test covers this.

## Subscription-quota pricing curve

Default curve (tunable):
- 0-70% used: effective $/1k tokens = 0
- 70-80%: 0.1× the pay-per-token rate for the equivalent route-target
- 80-90%: 0.3× pay-per-token
- 90-100%: 1.2× pay-per-token (demote below pay-per-token so routing drains the subscription gracefully)","acceptance":"1. `Upstream-serviceEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.
2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint upstream-services via route-target catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 upstream-services at different prices for the same route-target and asserts correct population.
3. Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each runner + each curve band.
4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 upstream-services with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 upstream-services with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).
5. Unknown-cost upstream-services are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost upstream-services are not starved, unknown-cost upstream-services are not demoted into last place.
6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent runner with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: "unknown"`.
7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude runner at 92% quota, seed an openrouter upstream-service with equivalent route-target at known cost → openrouter wins.
8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which upstream-service was picked and why.
9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).
10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.","labels":["routing","cost","routing-plan"],"bead":{"id":"agent-0dafc7f0","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"## Motivation

14. lefthook run pre-commit passes (including the new no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths rule).","closing_commit_sha":"0826fac9fb709179b22a74b24cf9108623b7ef20","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:16:44.121242708Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-06cbaa90","depends_on_id":"ddx-77eb084e","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:33:22Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-06cbaa90","depends_on_id":"ddx-5cc92777","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T07:58:30Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS

.ddx/beads.jsonl:8:{"closing_commit_sha":"81958ef5baab9362ad0e85b1c481c0c5a20a4c21","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:29:{"created_at":"2026-06-29T10:45:26.873700708Z","description":"PROBLEM
Observed on 2026-06-29 in this repo while draining the queue. Worker pid 4014720 hit 4 consecutive 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes within ~2h on different beads (ddx-9aa2151d, ddx-95d80f7e, ddx-1c865bdb, ddx-aef568e7) without any successful merge. Load average was 38-52 throughout (8-core host). After each transport failure the worker advances to the next ready bead and the cycle repeats; closed counter unchanged across the period.

Specific log signature:
  do response complete in <N>m
  tracker_lock section=durable_audit wait=1ms hold=<seconds>
  ✗ ddx-<id> → transport: exit status 1

Some attempts had very short responses (1m37s) suggesting codex died early; others completed long (32m+) then died at durable_audit. Common factor: the provider subprocess exits status 1, which DDx classifies as 'transport' (not tests_red, not no_changes, not timeout).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The outcome classifier maps any non-zero provider exit (codex/claude/etc.) to 'transport: exit status 1' regardless of cause (OOM, kill, network error mid-stream). Inspect cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around the outcome-mapping path for the codex/provider exit code (search 'transport: exit status'). The classifier does not distinguish 'load-killed' from 'genuine bug' from 'provider API error'.
- Under high system load (load avg > ~3-5x CPU count), the provider subprocess gets starved during the durable_audit / land phase (which is git+disk heavy), bumps into kernel OOM-killer or hits its own internal heartbeat/timeout, and exits non-zero. DDx then walks the queue trying the next bead which also dies, burning context+tokens with zero forward progress.
- There is no backoff/cooldown when transport failures cluster (e.g., 3+ in N minutes).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add load-pressure detection: before each claim, sample /proc/loadavg and compare to runtime.NumCPU(); if 5-min loadavg > k * cores (k=2-3 reasonable), emit a structured backoff observation and sleep proportional to overload before claiming.
2. Add transport-failure cooldown: when N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes occur within the last M minutes, the worker should pause for a longer interval (e.g., 5-15 min) before next claim instead of immediately consuming another bead.
3. Capture and surface the provider subprocess's exit reason where possible: check WaitStatus for signal-killed (SIGKILL → OOM-likely, SIGTERM → graceful), and distinguish in the outcome string (e.g., 'transport: provider killed by SIGKILL (likely OOM)').

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (outcome classifier + claim-loop pacing)
- cli/cmd/work.go (worker pacing/backoff)
- cli/internal/workerstatus/ (if load sampling needs a place)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not change the queue ordering.
- Do not change the durable_audit commit flow itself.
- Do not implement a general-purpose host load manager.

AC
1. New helper LoadPressureSnapshot() in cli/internal/workerstatus or cli/internal/agent returning normalized loadavg/cores; unit test covers >threshold and <threshold paths.
2. Worker pre-claim path checks the snapshot; when pressure exceeds k (default 2.5), logs a structured 'load_pressure_backoff' observation and sleeps before claim. Unit test asserts the path is invoked with k configurable.
3. Worker tracks last N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes by wall-clock timestamp; when 3 occur within 10m, the next claim is delayed by an extended cooldown (>=5m). Unit test simulates 3 failures within window and asserts the delay.
4. Outcome classifier distinguishes signal-killed (SIGKILL/SIGTERM) provider exits in the outcome string. Unit test asserts the distinct outcomes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/workerstatus/... -count=1 green.

  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.","events_attachment":"ddx-358f2457/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-358f2457","issue_type":"task","labels":["kind:refactor","phase:2","reconciled:no-changes-state","refactor"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go

.ddx/beads.jsonl:48:{"acceptance":"1. ddx work focus is read-only and exits successfully on an empty queue.
2. Human-readable output has separate sections for Requires human, Blocked/planning, Worker-ready summary, Worker recommendation, and Unknowns; ready implementation beads are not listed as primary intervention work by default.
3. --json returns stable keys human_required, blocked_or_planning, ready_summary, worker_recommendation, and unknowns.
4. Worker recommendation logic is conservative: it can suggest another worker when ready depth is high and active/in-progress capacity is observable, and it reports unknown capacity instead of inventing a blocker when signals are absent.
5. TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue covers needs_human/review-blocked and blocked/planning rows.
6. TestWorkFocusOmitsWorkerReadyBeadsByDefault covers the ready-versus-intervention distinction.
7. TestWorkFocusJSONIncludesUnknownHazards covers JSON unknown capacity/resource signals.
8. TestWorkFocusRecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue covers the conservative worker-start suggestion.
9. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue|TestWorkFocusOmitsWorkerReadyBeadsByDefault|TestWorkFocusJSONIncludesUnknownHazards|TestWorkFocusRecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue" -count=1 passes.
10. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"ed18389cfec7f125258baf96041ea301e1e7639c","created_at":"2026-05-07T18:24:57.534166681Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3c6ca63f","depends_on_id":"ddx-377ebf92","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T18:24:57Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

PARENT AND DEPS

The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `work` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.","id":"ddx-3ee4ddcf","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:docs","area:server","kind:refactor","nomenclature","phase:2","prevention","spec:FEAT-010"],"notes":"Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. This plan retains ddx agent compatibility aliases and legacy cli/internal/agent package targets, so it must not dispatch.","parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":4,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"cancelled","title":"agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature","updated_at":"2026-05-15T21:27:47.264393651Z"}

The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode model to every doc that references .ddx/ paths or project state. Each spec update added in this bead's commit cites principle-numbered tags so future readers see the link:
   - TD-031 bead state machine — "Storage anchors via ddxroot.Path(); see ddx-06cbaa90."
   - SD-019 multi-project-server-topology — Project identity from `host/owner/repo`; reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - SD-020 multi-machine-coordinator-topology — same.
   - ADR-021 operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path — reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - workers-sessions-operator-surface.md — worker status paths through ddxroot.Path().
   - plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture — FEAT-010 paths through resolver.
   - library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md — install precedence per ddx-937de9fc.
   - library/README.md — install topology overview.

NON-SCOPE
- New behaviours (every scenario in the matrix is already covered by ddx-06cbaa90 or ddx-937de9fc per-feature tests; this is the cross-mode equivalence layer).
- Migration tooling for existing in-tree repos (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree).

.ddx/beads.jsonl:63:{"acceptance":"1. All 15 children closed.
2. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run replaced by work.Worker.Drain (~30 lines).
3. 18 store-error-as-loop-exit smells eliminated; transient store error during outcome-commit logs and continues.
4. attempted/hookFailed maps deleted; pick derives exclusion from drain.result.
5. Routing preflight runs once at Drain startup, never per iteration.
6. ddx work mode semantics preserved: default is drain-until-no-ready-work; --once runs one ready bead then exits; --watch stays alive and sleeps for the idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist; --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error; no public compatibility alias remains for the removed polling flag.
7. ddx try <bead-id> CLI command exists.
8. File/identifier rename per package split complete; ExecuteBead* shims deleted.
9. JSONL event shapes (bead.result, execute-bead, review, routing, triage-decision, loop-error) unchanged - verified by C0 fixture baseline.
10. Server worker integration (cli/internal/server/) compiles and behaves identically except for the explicit once/drain/watch mode contract; tests green at every phase.
11. ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, and ddx-dc300be9 behaviors preserved per C5/C9/C10 absorption ACs.

FEAT-006 + FEAT-010 cross-refs updated to reflect new package paths and the mode + idle_interval worker-loop vocabulary.","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:58:24.112207288Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-12de0acc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:00:48Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e1fed08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:06Z"}],"description":"ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate terminal exits, 1 legacy idle-loop exit, 1 routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated across outcome paths. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick -> Gate -> Claim -> Execute -> Classify -> Reviewing -> Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   - Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   - Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  - Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes approximately 30 lines: pick -> guard -> claim -> attemptWithHeartbeat -> applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. The stdout/watch-mode beads (ddx-7d9e1e82, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, ddx-dc300be9) define the public work-loop contract this refactor must preserve. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming and public semantics

Package = try (not try_bead - Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface is ddx run / ddx try / ddx work. ddx work has three modes: once (one ready bead then exit), drain (default: run ready work until no execution-ready beads remain, then exit), and watch (stay alive indefinitely, sleep for idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist, and poll for newly arriving work). The removed polling flag is not preserved as a public compatibility alias; the durable vocabulary is mode plus idle_interval.

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation + downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes queue snapshot plumbing and human-review blocker pressure from ddx-7dcbd40b.
  C10 ddx work once/drain/watch modes + transient idle substate. Watch mode owns idle interval sleeps, per-idle queue status, and next-ready transition messaging from ddx-f9acb86d and ddx-8b7ed960.
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per package split + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)","events":[{"actor":"ddx bead doctor","body":"dependencies→removed dangling ddx-a8718bec (1 edge(s))

Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|PreClaim|RetryEscalation|PreClaimDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:74:{"acceptance":"1. New Vitest `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/design-tokens.test.ts`: the resolved Tailwind theme contains NO generic values (no `#8e35a3`, no `#4878c6`, no `#f9fafb`/`#ffffff` surface) and `accent-lever === '#3B5B7A'`; regenerating `design-tokens.json` from DESIGN.md keeps these.
2. Inter is actually LOADED: a Playwright assertion that `document.fonts` includes Inter (not merely declared) and `getComputedStyle(h1).fontFamily` resolves to the loaded family; re-baseline `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` snapshots.
3. The `semanticColors` alias layer is retained (class names unchanged); design:export remains the source of truth.
4. cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test && bun run test:e2e green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-05T17:51:32.412466203Z","description":"PROBLEM: Two issues mask the intended design system (`.stitch/DESIGN.md`: warm-paper brutalist). (1) `cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json` is the STALE generic Stitch export (`tertiary:#8e35a3` purple, `surface:#f9fafb`, Inter for all roles) — its warm palette only reaches the app because `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js:8-37` hardcodes `semanticColors` that override the JSON. So `bun run design:export` regenerates GENERIC values; a reader cannot tell which file is authoritative. (2) The typeface (`Inter`, tailwind.config.js:39-46) is SPECIFIED but never LOADED (no @font-face/@fontsource/Google Fonts anywhere) -> renders the OS system-sans fallback.

ROOT CAUSE: the design:export pipeline emits the generic default token DATA (not DESIGN.md's warm palette); and no font asset is imported. Per codex: `semanticColors` is the load-bearing alias layer (KEEP it) — only the generated token DATA is stale. After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go (RunState) persists only mechanical per-attempt fields (BeadID, AttemptID, WorktreePath, PID, candidate-cycle phase). No goal/stop-predicate/autonomy field.
- cli/cmd/work.go loop stop conditions are mechanical only (drained/blocked/deferred/no_progress/signal via ExitReason). There is no persisted operator goal that is re-evaluated each iteration.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a durable, optional goal object to the work-drive state (set once, e.g. `ddx work --goal <text|file>` or a persisted .ddx/run-state field): operator goal text + an explicit stop-predicate + autonomy posture.
- Re-load and surface it each work iteration so a restarted/compacted worker honors it without re-injection. This subsumes the old 'make the autonomy rule stick under a declared drain' item — the autonomy/stop posture lives here, not in HELIX docs.
- Keep it ORTHOGONAL to the bead queue: the queue is WHAT work; the goal is the stop condition + posture. Document why both exist.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing or power selection.
- Do not change bead lifecycle statuses.
- Do not implement natural-language goal evaluation beyond a simple, testable predicate (e.g. 'queue empty AND <named gate> green').

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped.
