ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS

.ddx/beads.jsonl:97:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
5. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
6. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
7. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.
8. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.

(Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this lands.","events_attachment":"ddx-937de9fc/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-937de9fc","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","initiative:zero-footprint","kind:feature","phase:3","principle:skills-personas","reconciled:no-changes-state"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","notes":"Superseded by ddx-eaf02420 and ADR-027 (2026-06-15 amendment). The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T164659-abc25e07","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":285426,"output_tokens":2755,"total_tokens":288181,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":34343,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=288181 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"The bead is too large for this execution depth and cannot be cleanly split further here. A test shows the assembled prompt is unchanged when the file is absent. 3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes; go build ./... succeeds.","created_at":"2026-06-28T03:08:03.330496989Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators re-paste hard per-project invariants ('do not delete anything from redpanda', 'never run perf tests on GitHub runners', 'do not touch the NAT') as prose every dispatch and every compaction, because there is no per-project guardrails file spliced into the load-bearing guardrail block the executor controls. Guardrails are currently static global consts.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (~1945-2065): guardrails are hardcoded const prompt strings (instrCoreConstraints, instrBeadOverride, the numbered load-bearing guardrails). No reader splices a per-project file. The only per-project surface (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) is harness-loaded prose, not part of the executor-controlled guardrail block.

PROPOSED FIX
- Read an OPTIONAL project-root file (.ddx/guardrails.md) and splice its contents into the execute-bead guardrail assembly so per-project invariants are injected on every dispatch (and therefore survive compaction automatically, since they are re-injected each iteration).
- Absent file = no behavioral change.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the existing static guardrail consts' wording.
- Do not add AGENTS.md parsing or change how the harness loads AGENTS.md.
- Do not implement per-bead overrides (this is per-project only).

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.","id":"ddx-c015b098","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:execute-bead","kind:feature","source:session-harvest-2026-06-27"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"Inject per-project .ddx/guardrails.md into the execute-bead guardrail block","updated_at":"2026-06-28T03:08:03.330496989Z"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-999eee75/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-999eee75 (Stage E2: GraphQL bounded text egress)
================================================================================

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------

- Read all in-scope files (schema.graphql, resolver_documents.go,
  resolver_agent.go) plus generated.go, models.go, gqlgen.yml.
- Read the FEAT-022 §10/§11 invariants and the existing E1 work in
  cli/internal/server/server.go and cli/internal/server/state_executions.go
  (uses evidence.ClampOutput + truncated/original_bytes pattern).
- Read the existing TestServerBoundedEgress in
  cli/internal/server/bounded_egress_test.go to understand the fixture
  pattern (lower serverInlineCapBytes → 256, body sized 2× cap, assert
  length cap+marker, truncated, originalBytes).
- Determined the schema-edit + resolver-edit footprint that would be
  required:
    1. schema.graphql: add `truncated: Boolean!` and `originalBytes: Int!`
       to type Document and type AgentSession; introduce a new type
       WorkerPrompt { text: String!, truncated: Boolean!,
       originalBytes: Int! } and change Query.workerPrompt return type
       from String! to WorkerPrompt!.
    2. resolver_documents.go DocumentByPath: route resolved file content
       through evidence.ClampOutput; populate Truncated + OriginalBytes
       on the returned *Document.
    3. resolver_agent.go WorkerPrompt and AgentSession: same treatment
       for prompt body and for agentSession Prompt+Response (matching
       the REST handler's "OriginalBytes is sum of prompt+response"
       pattern from server.go:2472–2480 + bounded_egress_test.go:196–199).
    4. New TestGraphQLBoundedEgress in this package with one subtest per
       in-scope resolver, using newGQLHandler + a custom testStateProvider
       that returns oversized fixtures.
    5. Run gqlgen to refresh generated.go and models.go.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------

The acceptance contract requires:

    cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate

to exit 0. With the current package layout this is not achievable, and
it is **not caused by any edit this bead would introduce** — the failure
reproduces from the pristine base revision (e6cad0d6) before any
schema or resolver change:

    $ git checkout e6cad0d6 -- cli/internal/server/graphql/
    $ cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
    ./resolver_feat008.go:36:28: method mutationResolver.BeadClose already declared at ./resolver.go:165:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:58:28: method mutationResolver.WorkerDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:244:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:77:28: method mutationResolver.StartWorker already declared at ./resolver.go:263:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:107:28: method mutationResolver.StopWorker already declared at ./resolver.go:293:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:115:28: method mutationResolver.PluginDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:301:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:147:28: method mutationResolver.ComparisonDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:333:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:192:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaBind already declared at ./resolver.go:378:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:206:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaCreate already declared at ./resolver.go:392:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:216:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaUpdate already declared at ./resolver.go:402:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:226:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaDelete already declared at ./resolver.go:412:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:226:28: too many errors
    exit status 1

ROOT CAUSE
----------
gqlgen v0.17.89 (the version pinned in cli/go.mod) is configured in
single-file resolver layout via cli/internal/server/graphql/gqlgen.yml:

    resolver:
      filename: resolver.go
      package: graphql
      type: Resolver

In this layout the resolvergen plugin
(/home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/plugin/resolvergen/resolver.go,
generateSingleFile) walks every required resolver field and calls
rewriter.GetMethodBody(structName, GoFieldName) to recover an existing
implementation. The rewriter (internal/rewrite/rewriter.go GetPrevDecl)
scans the entire package via `packages.Load(NeedSyntax|NeedTypes)`, so
it finds existing implementations regardless of which file they live in
— but the generator then writes the recovered body into a NEW method on
`*mutationResolver` in the configured resolver.go. The original
declaration in resolver_feat008.go (or any other sibling file) is left
in place. Both files now define the same method on the same receiver,
which is a hard Go compile error.

Concretely: ~9 mutation methods (BeadClose, WorkerDispatch, StartWorker,
StopWorker, PluginDispatch, ComparisonDispatch, PersonaBind,
PersonaCreate, PersonaUpdate, PersonaDelete, …) live in
resolver_feat008.go (1193 LOC, also containing helpers + types like
paletteResultCap and the pluginActionMu mutex that are not gqlgen
artefacts). Every one of them gets duplicated into resolver.go on each
gqlgen run. The first run from a clean checkout already breaks the
build; subsequent runs see the broken state and add the resolver_agent.go
query methods (Workers/AgentSessions/etc.) on top, compounding the
problem.

This bug predates Stage E2. It is the product of single-file gqlgen
layout coexisting with hand-split sibling resolver files, and it has
existed at least since resolver_feat008.go was introduced (FEAT-008
work). Earlier "regeneration" commits (e.g. f912445f
feat(providers): inline 24h sparkline) only touched generated.go +
models.go without rerunning gqlgen end-to-end against this resolver
package — those edits were applied by hand or by reverting resolver.go
post-regen.

NO IN-SCOPE WORKAROUND
----------------------
The bead's named scope (schema.graphql, resolver_documents.go,
resolver_agent.go, generated.go, models.go) cannot break this. The only
fixes that would make `gqlgen generate` exit 0 require structural
changes outside scope:

  - Consolidating resolver_feat008.go's methods into resolver.go
    (deleting the duplicate-in-resolver_feat008.go set after each
    regen). This is a ~1k-LOC restructure that crosses several
    unrelated FEAT-008 / persona / worker dispatch concerns.
  - Switching gqlgen to layout: follow-schema with a per-schema-file
    resolver dir, requiring a renamed resolver tree and fresh stubs.
  - Tagging resolver_feat008.go with a build constraint that hides it
    from `packages.Load` during generation, then reintroducing it for
    runtime — fragile and makes generation a non-default path
    (`GOFLAGS=-tags=…`), which would itself break the AC's literal
    "exits 0 from cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run …" wording.

Either fix is a refactor of the resolver package's organisational
contract and is well outside the surface this bead is scoped to. The
bead's instruction "Do not modify files outside the scope the bead
description names" applies to resolver_feat008.go.

I considered a partial commit that:
  - Adds the schema and resolver edits this bead names.
  - Hand-edits generated.go and models.go to match what gqlgen would
    have produced.
  - Adds TestGraphQLBoundedEgress with the required subtests.
  - Leaves the gqlgen-exits-0 AC unmet.

That is functionally adequate but it would commit red against an
explicit AC checkbox ("regeneration succeeds cleanly") and would push
the "fix the gqlgen workflow" decision onto the reviewer with no
documented rationale. Per the bead instructions ("a well-justified
no_changes is better than a bad commit"), the right answer is to
surface the blocker now so the next attempt can either (i) explicitly
expand scope to consolidate resolvers, (ii) update the AC to drop the
"exit 0" requirement and accept hand-curated generated.go/models.go,
or (iii) split a precursor bead that fixes the gqlgen workflow first.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------

Pick one and write it into the bead before re-queuing:

  Option A — Precursor bead first.
    Create a new bead "graphql: consolidate resolvers so gqlgen generate
    is reproducible" that moves the method bodies of resolver_feat008.go
    (and any other sibling resolver_*.go files that hold gqlgen-managed
    mutation/query methods) into resolver.go and trims the siblings down
    to non-gqlgen helpers (constants, types, vars, plain functions).
    Verify gqlgen generate exits 0 from a clean checkout. Then re-run
    Stage E2 — schema + clamp + tests — on top.

  Option B — Relax the AC.
    Change the Stage E2 AC from "go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen
    generate exits 0" to "generated.go and models.go expose the new
    fields and the package builds cleanly". This is consistent with how
    f912445f and other prior schema-touching commits actually shipped.
    Then a single Stage E2 commit can carry the schema edit + hand-
    matched generated.go/models.go diffs + resolver edits + tests.

  Option C — Switch layout.
    Move resolver: { filename: resolver.go } to layout: follow-schema
    with a dedicated resolver dir. This is a larger configuration
    change that would reshape every resolver_*.go file in the package
    and is also out of E2's scope.

The schema diff, resolver clamp logic, and test fixtures I already
designed in section (a) are ready to apply on top of any of those —
they are the easy part. The hard part is the gqlgen workflow contract,
which Stage E2 does not own.

VERIFICATION ARTEFACTS
----------------------
- Reproduction of the gqlgen failure from base rev e6cad0d6 (commands
  + raw output) is captured above.
- Inspection of resolvergen single-file behaviour:
  /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/plugin/resolvergen/resolver.go
  generateSingleFile, lines 54–120. Combined with
  /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/internal/rewrite/rewriter.go
  GetPrevDecl + RemainingSource (lines 71–134, 196+), shows the
  duplicate-write-back behaviour for cross-file method definitions.
- The pre-existing E1 implementation
  (cli/internal/server/server.go:54–65 mcpText helper,
  :988, :2169, :2472–2480; cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:213–221)
  documents the truncated/original_bytes contract Stage E2 must mirror;
  this is the pattern I would follow once the gqlgen workflow blocker
  is resolved.

No commit was created for this bead.

expand scope to consolidate resolvers, (ii) update the AC to drop the
"exit 0" requirement and accept hand-curated generated.go/models.go,
or (iii) split a precursor bead that fixes the gqlgen workflow first.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------

Pick one and write it into the bead before re-queuing:

  Option A — Precursor bead first.
    Create a new bead "graphql: consolidate resolvers so gqlgen generate
    is reproducible" that moves the method bodies of resolver_feat008.go
    (and any other sibling resolver_*.go files that hold gqlgen-managed
    mutation/query methods) into resolver.go and trims the siblings down
    to non-gqlgen helpers (constants, types, vars, plain functions).
    Verify gqlgen generate exits 0 from a clean checkout. Then re-run
    Stage E2 — schema + clamp + tests — on top.

  Option B — Relax the AC.
    Change the Stage E2 AC from "go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen
    generate exits 0" to "generated.go and models.go expose the new
    fields and the package builds cleanly". This is consistent with how
    f912445f and other prior schema-touching commits actually shipped.
    Then a single Stage E2 commit can carry the schema edit + hand-
    matched generated.go/models.go diffs + resolver edits + tests.

  Option C — Switch layout.
    Move resolver: { filename: resolver.go } to layout: follow-schema
    with a dedicated resolver dir. This is a larger configuration
    change that would reshape every resolver_*.go file in the package
    and is also out of E2's scope.

The schema diff, resolver clamp logic, and test fixtures I already
designed in section (a) are ready to apply on top of any of those —
they are the easy part. The hard part is the gqlgen workflow contract,
which Stage E2 does not own.

VERIFICATION ARTEFACTS
----------------------
- Reproduction of the gqlgen failure from base rev e6cad0d6 (commands
  + raw output) is captured above.
- Inspection of resolvergen single-file behaviour:
  /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/plugin/resolvergen/resolver.go
  generateSingleFile, lines 54–120. Combined with
  /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/internal/rewrite/rewriter.go
  GetPrevDecl + RemainingSource (lines 71–134, 196+), shows the
  duplicate-write-back behaviour for cross-file method definitions.
- The pre-existing E1 implementation
  (cli/internal/server/server.go:54–65 mcpText helper,
  :988, :2169, :2472–2480; cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:213–221)
  documents the truncated/original_bytes contract Stage E2 must mirror;
  this is the pattern I would follow once the gqlgen workflow blocker
  is resolved.

No commit was created for this bead.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:52:{"id":"corpus-368f5152b39b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?

Three alternatives:

(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.

(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the work / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.

Additionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<random>-<hex> (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.

Scope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-d3a4f89c","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:56:{"id":"corpus-399a45730bb9","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI upstream-service error aborts agent without classification","description":"## Symptom

Sessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:

```
agent: upstream-service error: received error while streaming: {"message":"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI"}
```

Observed against `surface=embedded-openai`. The agent surfaces this as a generic "upstream-service error" and aborts the session.

## Why it matters

This is a **server-capability** error — the local OpenAI-compat server (probably mlx_lm / lmstudio variant) cannot handle a quantized rotating KV cache for the route-target+config combination. The user can fix it (turn off KV quant; use a different cache type; pick a different route-target). Today the error is opaque, so the user just sees a stream abort and may retry endlessly.

## Proposed fix

In `upstream-service/openai` (or wherever the streaming SSE error is decoded):

1. Detect the `NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI` substring (or any `NotImplementedError` from the upstream-service) and classify it as a structured error, not a transport error.
2. Emit a clear human message: "Local server does not support quantized rotating KV cache for this route-target. Disable KV quantization on the server, or use a route-target/config it supports."
3. Mark the error non-retryable in the routing layer — endless retries waste compute and the failure is deterministic.
4. Optional: add a routing hint so this upstream-service+route-target combo is skipped on subsequent dispatches in the same session.

## Related

Counterpart bead being filed in lucebox-hub/dflash to add this as a known failure mode the bench should be able to provoke deliberately (it currently passes `enable_thinking: false` to avoid the related "reasoning stall" path, but doesn't surface KV-cache-incompatibility paths).","acceptance":"1. 2. The error is non-retryable in the routing layer — agent does not silently retry on the same upstream-service+route-target.
3. A unit test fixture replays a recorded SSE stream containing this error and asserts the structured classification.
4. library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:62:{"id":"corpus-42009f026676","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"correctness: anthropic streaming usage loses input tokens across deltas","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 51f778b.
File: upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:236, upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:275, stream_consume.go:84
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Anthropic streaming emits input tokens on the initial message_start delta and output/cache tokens on later message_delta events, but consumeStream replaces resp.Usage on each delta instead of accumulating it. In the default streaming path, the final Response therefore drops the earlier input token counts and retains only the last usage fragment. Anthropic's streaming code also never sets Total, so even if the fragments were merged the run-level total token count would remain zero. This leaves llm.response, session.end, and any cost/accounting derived from streaming Anthropic responses inconsistent with the non-streaming upstream-service path.
Suggested fix: Accumulate streaming usage fragments in consumeStream instead of overwriting them, and have the Anthropic streaming path emit enough data to compute a correct final TokenUsage.Total, or emit the full merged usage on the terminal delta. Add a regression test that simulates Anthropic-style split usage deltas and asserts the final Response and emitted response event preserve input, output, cache, and total token counts.","acceptance":"For Anthropic streaming runs, token usage from message_start and message_delta is merged into the final Response and llm.response event; Input, Output, CacheRead, and CacheWrite are preserved; Total equals Input+Output; a regression test covers split usage deltas","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-24b844fb","title":"correctness: anthropic streaming usage loses input tokens across deltas","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 51f778b.

Add a regression test that simulates Anthropic-style split usage deltas and asserts the final Response and emitted response event preserve input, output, cache, and total token counts.","acceptance":"For Anthropic streaming runs, token usage from message_start and message_delta is merged into the final Response and llm.response event; Input, Output, CacheRead, and CacheWrite are preserved; Total equals Input+Output; a regression test covers split usage deltas","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}
library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:63:{"id":"corpus-42801b4c2e9b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30
  const DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute

This is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every upstream-service regardless of route-target class. It is set on every service path:
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout

The comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'

But that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:
  const DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute

The 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.

Concrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):

1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)
   - status: no_changes
   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)
   - log: 'WARN upstream-service error, retrying attempt=1 err="upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s" delay=1s'
   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.

2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)
   - All 3 hit 'upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt
   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each

Why thinking route-targets hit this:
qwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning route-targets can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the runner considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the runner is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.

Why arbitrary timeouts are not helping:

a) Same value for cloud upstream-services (claude, codex) and local upstream-services (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking route-targets routinely need >15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.

b) No way to opt out of the cap per upstream-service, per route-target, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.

c) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.

d) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking route-target the timeout doesn't mean 'broken upstream-service', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","acceptance":"AC1. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[upstream-service].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per upstream-service.

AC2. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-tier via the route-target-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking route-target (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat route-targets keep the 15-min default.

AC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.

AC4. When Upstream-serviceTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').

AC5. work / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.

AC6. Default for known-thinking-route-target surfaces (upstream-service.type=lmstudio AND route-target name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:runner","kind:design-flaw"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-2c63bb95","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:93:{"id":"corpus-612aeaa9052a","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard","description":"Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.

Context:
- A real PTY session against installed Gemini CLI 0.38.2 showed /stats route-target only reports route-target/session usage after an API call. It did not show quota remaining/limit/reset for the active concrete route-target.
- The same PTY session showed /route-target manage is the quota surface. Observed output included: Flash 4% used, reset 9:13 PM (23h 46m); Flash Lite 0% used, reset 9:27 PM (24h); Pro 100% used.
- Upstream Gemini CLI docs say /stats route-target shows token counts and quota information, but the installed CLI renders quota in the route-target management dialog. Treat both /stats route-target and /route-target manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.
- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by route-target/tier, with freshness and source recorded.

In-scope files:
- internal/runneres/gemini/ or internal/runneres/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.
- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.
- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.
- docs/helix/02-design/primary-runner-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/runner-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.

Out-of-scope:
- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.
- Do not promote Gemini to primary/auto-routing unless routing consumes the parsed quota cache and blocks exhausted tiers.
- Do not change Codex or Claude quota behavior except shared helper refactors required by the Gemini parser.","acceptance":"1. Add a Gemini PTY quota probe or cassette-backed parser that can parse /route-target manage output containing tier/route-target usage percentages and reset times, including exhausted tiers such as Pro 100% used.
2. Persist parsed Gemini quota evidence in a durable cache with source, captured_at, freshness, per-tier/route-target used percent or remaining/limit, and reset time.
3. ListRunneres reports Gemini quota only from parsed quota evidence, never from auth/account freshness alone. Missing or stale Gemini quota evidence is not ok.
4. Automatic routing does not select Gemini when quota evidence is missing/stale or the selected tier/route-target is exhausted; if promotion is enabled, it is gated by fresh parsed quota evidence.
5. Add tests covering the observed PTY output: Flash 4% used with reset, Flash Lite 0% used with reset, and Pro 100% used/exhausted.
6. go test ./... passes.","labels":["area:runner","area:gemini","kind:feature"],"bead":{"id":"agent-37659612","title":"Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.

- Upstream Gemini CLI docs say /stats route-target shows token counts and quota information, but the installed CLI renders quota in the route-target management dialog. Treat both /stats route-target and /route-target manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.
- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by route-target/tier, with freshness and source recorded.

In-scope files:
- internal/runneres/gemini/ or internal/runneres/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.
- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.
- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.
- docs/helix/02-design/primary-runner-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/runner-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.

Out-of-scope:
- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.
- Do not promote Gemini to primary/auto-routing unless routing consumes the parsed quota cache and blocks exhausted tiers.
- Do not change Codex or Claude quota behavior except shared helper refactors required by the Gemini parser.","acceptance":"1. Add a Gemini PTY quota probe or cassette-backed parser that can parse /route-target manage output containing tier/route-target usage percentages and reset times, including exhausted tiers such as Pro 100% used.
2. Persist parsed Gemini quota evidence in a durable cache with source, captured_at, freshness, per-tier/route-target used percent or remaining/limit, and reset time.

- Codex: native session JSONL adapter for current quota/headroom and recent usage totals
- Claude: stats-cache adapter for historical usage

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:134:{"id":"corpus-906830fa2998","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance route-target that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","acceptance":"ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"bead":{"id":"hx-63ac1232","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:135:{"id":"corpus-91b372c1834c","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Decision: tmux quota refresh ownership — agent owns it; remove DDx routing_signal_tmux.go duplicate","description":"Per codex review: DDx routing_signal_tmux.go (with ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux, ReadCodexQuotaViaTmux, ANSI strip, output parsers) is duplicated by agent internal/runneres/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go (commit 8099293). DDx should not keep parallel tmux scrape parsers long-term.

Two paths:
A. Agent owns tmux scrape; DDx triggers refresh via service.HealthCheck(runner target). The agent does the tmux invocation internally; DDx never shells out tmux. Delete DDx routing_signal_tmux.go.
B. Remove the DDx-side doctor refresh path entirely. Quota state populates lazily via the agents normal probe cadence; doctor --routing reads from cache only.

Recommendation: A. service.HealthCheck(runner) already triggers a fresh probe internally (per the implementation report from agent-3afb76bb). The tmux refresh becomes implicit when DDx asks for HealthCheck on claude/codex. No new contract surface needed.

After this decision: routing_signal_tmux.go can delete from DDx (folded into the file-delete cascade); doctor --routing migration uses service.HealthCheck instead of explicit RefreshXQuotaViaTmuxForWorkDir.","acceptance":"- [ ] Decision recorded with rationale

The observability_workdir.go wrappers currently build a minimal Runner via NewRunner(cfg) and call its observability methods (ProbeRunnerState, LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux). This keeps Runner alive as an internal implementation detail.

Goal: lift the file-reading logic OUT of Runner methods into free functions (or just inline into observability_workdir.go). Then Runner can finally die, and the 5 file-delete beads cascade.

Key insight: the observability data DDx surfaces lives in DDx-managed paths (session JSONLs in cfg.SessionLogDir, quota caches in agent module - need to call into agent for quota refresh). These are file-read operations, not Runner-state operations.

Files involved:
- cli/internal/agent/observability_workdir.go — currently uses Runner-build pattern; rewrite to direct file reads
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go — extract file-reading logic from methods then delete those methods (Runner.ProbeRunnerState, Runner.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, Runner.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, Runner.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux). library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:162:{"id":"corpus-b32747c53ca5","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Add route-target-route config schema and backend compatibility translation","description":"Implement the config-layer replacement described in FEAT-004, SD-005, and plan-2026-04-10-route-target-first-routing.md. Preserve direct named-upstream-service config. Load legacy backends/default_backend as deprecated compatibility input, translate them into internal route-target-route records at config-load time, and emit a deprecation warning path that the CLI can surface without breaking existing users. Validation should fail early for unknown upstream-services, empty candidate lists, and unknown route names before agent.Run() starts. 3. Persistent file context — ordered consistently (e.g. alphabetically) within a session
4. Conversation history — append-only, never reorder
5. Current user turn — always last

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:186:{"id":"corpus-cbc4ba9c4924","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Prevent work from launching open epics by default","description":"<context-digest>
Alpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent work --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current work implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent work` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.
2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.
3. Run `ddx agent work --once --json`.
4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.

## Observed gap
`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `work` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.

## Why this matters
Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.","acceptance":"`ddx agent work` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-b44ca394","title":"Prevent work from launching open epics by default","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"<context-digest>

</context-digest>

The current integration suite proves simple read/edit flows against LM Studio, but it does not exercise the broader shipped tool surface that FEAT-002 now claims. Add at least one environment-gated route-target-backed acceptance scenario that drives a real upstream-service through navigation and editing work using the benchmark-oriented tools (`glob`, `grep`, `ls`, `patch`, and/or `task`) rather than only `read`/`edit`.

The test should:
- create a disposable workspace with a small but non-trivial file graph
- prompt the route-target to discover a target via navigation tools, make a change, and confirm the result
- assert on actual tool-call logs and resulting files, not just the final assistant text
- skip explicitly when the configured integration environment is unavailable

Workflow (non-negotiable, prevents half-done landings):

1. BEFORE touching any code, run the current (pre-collapse) CLI review
   path against a committed fixture bead/rev/refs and write the

   fixture: converge on agent.reviewXMLEscape's behavior unless a

   case extend BuildReviewPrompt with a typed flag parameter (e.g.
   EscapeQuotes bool). Do NOT keep a duplicate escape helper.

   the golden file when run against the same fixture inputs.

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (only if a typed flag is

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:215:{"id":"corpus-e692e0e87746","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"feat: compaction user-message tail re-inclusion after compact","description":"<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>gofmt · go vet · golangci-lint · gosec · govulncheck · go test · go test -race · testify/assert for assertions</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 — OTel GenAI spans and JSONL session log are separate observability surfaces</adrs>
<governing>SD-006 §Summary Injection — the compaction summary replaces all compacted messages. SD-006 §Codex research — keeps up to 20K tokens of recent user messages alongside summary. FEAT-001 AC-FEAT-001-06 — compaction no-fit paths must fail closed.</governing>
</context-digest>

Currently compaction/compaction.go replaces compacted history with only the summary message. Codex retains the most recent real user messages (up to a token budget) alongside the summary so the route-target retains actual request history.

What to change in compaction/compaction.go (or the Compactor function):
- After generating the summary, collect real user-role messages from the tail of the pre-compaction history (exclude system prefix messages, previous compaction summaries, and tool result messages — only real user messages).
- Accumulate messages from newest to oldest until a configurable token budget is exhausted (default: 20000 tokens, chars/4 heuristic). Per SD-006 §Overlong individual user messages, truncate individual messages that exceed the budget rather than dropping them.
- The replacement history becomes: [summary_message, ...tail_user_messages] ordered so the summary is first and user messages follow in chronological order.
- Expose the budget as a new field UserMessageTailTokens int in CompactionConfig (default 20000; 0 = disabled).
- All existing compaction tests must still pass.
- Add test TestCompact_UserMessageTailReInclusion verifying tail messages appear in replacement history.

Governing specs: SD-006, FEAT-001.","acceptance":"go test ./... passes; CompactionConfig gains UserMessageTailTokens field (default 20000); new test TestCompact_UserMessageTailReInclusion exists and verifies that after compaction the replacement history contains both the summary message and the most recent real user messages up to the token budget, in chronological order","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:lib"],"bead":{"id":"agent-d1e7b4a8","title":"feat: compaction user-message tail re-inclusion after compact","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"<context-digest>

- picompat/settings.go: reads ~/.pi/agent/settings.json → default upstream-service/route-target

Pi upstream-service name → agent mapping per SD-007:
- anthropic → anthropic (type: anthropic)
- openai-codex → openai (type: openai-compat, default URL)
- openrouter → openrouter (type: openai-compat)

- Unsupported api field values
- headers with !command resolution

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:229:{"id":"corpus-f546b016a732","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Implement deterministic script runner for orchestration stress testing","description":"The work / execute-bead / land-coordinator system cannot be tested end-to-end today because every "fake" agent in the codebase returns a canned Result{} without actually writing files or making commits. `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go` is a prompt-response dictionary replayer — it returns canned stdout based on prompt hash but does not touch the filesystem or git. `fakeAgentRunner` and `fakeExecuteBeadGit` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go` are in-memory mocks that track expected call counts. Neither produces actual commits that the orchestration layer can rebase, merge, or land.

The consequence: every test that claims to cover concurrency, serialization, or git orchestration uses mocked git and therefore cannot catch merge-race, rebase-conflict, or coordinator-serialization bugs. `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace` at execute_bead_orchestrator_test.go:228 is particularly misleading — it uses separate orchTestGitOps instances per worker so the shared-writer invariant is never exercised. The test passes because no shared state exists to race on.

## What this bead builds

A new deterministic `script` runner (cli/internal/agent/script.go, mirroring cli/internal/agent/virtual.go):

- Registered alongside claude/codex/agent/virtual in cli/internal/agent/registry.go
- Always available (no binary probe required)
- Takes a directive file (passed via RunOptions.Route-target or a new RunOptions.ScriptFile field — either works)
- Reads the directive file, executes each line against opts.WorkDir as real filesystem + git operations
- Returns a real Result{ExitCode, Output} that the loop and orchestrator can observe

## Directive grammar

Line-based, comments start with `#`. Each directive performs a real side effect:

```
append-line <path> <text>          # append text to file (create if missing)
create-file <path> <content>       # create or overwrite file
delete-file <path>                 # rm -f path
modify-line <path> <regex> <repl>  # sed-style in-place substitution
run <shell-command>                # exec in WorkDir with clean env
commit <message>                   # git add -A && git commit -m <message>
set-exit <code>                    # final exit code (default 0)
no-op                              # no commits, no files, exit 0
fail-during <index>                # synthetic failure at the Nth directive
sleep-ms <n>                       # deterministic delay (for scheduling tests)
```

Directives run in order. Environment variables DDX_BEAD_ID, DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, DDX_WORKER_ID are interpolated into text arguments so directives can be parameterized. Example:

```
append-line tests/smoke.txt "hello from ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
commit "smoke: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
```

## Invocation

From the CLI: `ddx agent run --runner=script --route-target=./testdata/append-smoke.txt --prompt=/dev/null`

Programmatically from a test:
```go
runner := agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{Runner: "script"})
result, err := runner.Run(agent.RunOptions{
    WorkDir: wtPath,
    Route-target:   "testdata/append-smoke.txt",
    Correlation: map[string]string{"bead_id": beadID, "attempt_id": attemptID},
})
```

## Implementation notes

- Mirror the dispatch pattern in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:81 where `virtual` is intercepted
- Always-available in Discover() same as virtual + agent
- No external dependencies beyond what the agent package already uses (os, os/exec for git)
- Estimated size: ~200 lines of implementation + ~200 lines of tests
- Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go must prove each directive actually performs its claimed side effect on a real temp git repo

## What this enables

Every test case in beads B (integration tests) and C (server runWorker regression test) depends on this runner existing. Without it, the land-coordinator redesign ships behind whatever real claude happens to do on the day the test runs.","acceptance":"New file cli/internal/agent/script.go implements RunScript(opts) that reads a directive file, executes directives against opts.WorkDir using real filesystem and git operations, and returns a real Result. The runner is registered in cli/internal/agent/registry.go as `script` and marked always-available in Discover(). Directive grammar supports at minimum: append-line, create-file, delete-file, commit, set-exit, no-op, sleep-ms, fail-during. Environment variable interpolation supports ${DDX_BEAD_ID}, ${DDX_ATTEMPT_ID}, ${DDX_WORKER_ID}. .ddx/attachments/ddx-af187382/events.jsonl:6:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes
rationale: ddx-af187382 — agent: migrate internal helpers off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions

Second-attempt no_changes after re-analysis with the EXPANDED scope (option b
authorized 2026-04-26).

## What is done

- Confirmed prior worker's analysis (attempt 1, base 12a2b57f). The runtime-lint
  pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml:164–186) filters violations to *staged* files.
  Any staged Go file that mentions a RunOptions / CompareOptions / QuorumOptions
  composite literal or function-parameter signature fails the gate.
- Inventoried all ref sites under the expanded scope. 38 .go files touch the
  three legacy types (excluding tools/lint testdata). Of those:
    Production:
      cli/internal/agent/grade.go (1 site — trivial, AC item 1)
      cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith,
        runFixtureHarnessViaRunner — public API surface)
      cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go (RunCompareViaService,
        RunCompareWithAgent, RunCompareWith, RunQuorumViaService,
        RunQuorumWithAgent, RunQuorumWith, RunBenchmarkViaService,
        RunBenchmarkWithAgent, RunBenchmarkWith, runCompareArmWith,
        defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunFunc, BenchmarkArmsToCompare)
      cli/internal/agent/runner.go (Run, RunWithConfig, BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
        resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock, resolvePrompt,
        TestProviderConnectivity — RunOptions is the central type)
      cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go,
        claude_stream.go, virtual.go, script.go (all consume RunOptions)
      cli/internal/agent/session_index.go (SessionIndexEntryFromResult signature)
      cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go, models.go (lesser uses)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647 dispatchAgentRun
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531 dispatchReviewRun
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462 (RunViaService) and :1243 (RunBenchmarkViaService)
    Tests (must be migrated to keep build green per AC item 6/7):
      cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go (15+ BuildArgs sites)
      cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go (3)
      cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go (6)
      cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (3)
      cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (2)
      cli/internal/agent/models_test.go (1)
      cli/internal/agent/run_internal_test.go, agent_run_runtime_test.go,
        execute_bead_runtime_test.go, execute_bead_review_test.go,
        execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go, execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go,
        execute_bead_e2e_test.go, execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_config_test.go, agent_quorum_config_test.go,
        agent_execute_bead_test.go, agent_compare_config_test.go,
        cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go
      cli/cmd/command_factory.go (production), cli/internal/config/testconfig.go,
        cli/internal/exec/types.go (mostly comments)

  Net surface: ~4,000 LoC of net change across the agent package, the cmd
  package, the server package, the exec package, the config package, plus
  ~10,000 LoC of test code that constructs the legacy types or accepts them
  as helper parameters. Every public-API surface change cascades to its
  callers, including BuildArgs (15+ test call sites) and
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult (consumed by session-index machinery and the
  oversize-prompt ingress test).

## What is blocking

The migration is too large for a single execute-bead worker window even with
the expanded scope. Three concrete reasons:

1. **Per-bead size budget.** SD-024 §Migration discipline (lines 234–256)
   explicitly mandates "<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time,
   under $10 of model spend" per bead, with a footnote that "Anything that
   wants to 'do the whole loop migration in one shot' is sized wrong and
   must be split." This bead's expanded scope is ~4,000 production LoC plus
   ~10,000 test LoC — 8x to 28x the SD-mandated budget. The previous attempt
   spent 16.6 minutes and $6.75 producing no_changes; even doubling that
   budget cannot produce a green tree across this surface in one pass.

2. **The migration is not one atomic change but a sequence of seam swaps.**
   Each affected public API (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,
   RunCompareViaService → RunCompareWithConfigViaService, BuildArgs's
   RunOptions parameter, SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter,
   RunFunc closure type) has its own caller graph. SD-024's own discipline
   (lines 248–256) prescribes per-caller migration beads precisely so each
   step leaves the tree green and lint-clean. Bundling them into one bead
   re-creates the bug class the SD's migration discipline was written to
   avoid: a half-finished refactor with the lint gate firing on the
   intermediate state, where the worker either has to disable hooks (forbidden)
   or revert most of the work to commit anything.

3. **Test-file lint cascade.** The runtime-lint pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml
   pattern 2) flags any staged Go file containing a RunOptions{} / CompareOptions{}
   / QuorumOptions{} composite literal. Migrating BuildArgs's signature
   alone (to take a non-RunOptions parameter) re-stages every one of the
   15+ agent_test.go test files, and the gate fires on each. The same
   cascade fires when SessionIndexEntryFromResult's signature changes
   (re-staging session_index_test.go), when RunFunc's closure type changes
   (re-staging compare/quorum/benchmark tests), and so on for each public-API
   seam swap. Every signature change requires migrating its consumers in
   the *same commit* — but the consumers' test files already contain
   composite literals from sibling test cases that aren't in this bead's
   AC. Those literals stay valid (BuildArgs still works) but the gate fires
   because the file is now staged. Untangling this requires touching every
   composite-literal site in every staged test file — hence the ~10,000 LoC
   of test work above.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

Per SD-024 §Stage 2 (lines 281–286) and §Migration discipline (lines 222–256),
this bead should be split along the seam-swap boundaries the SD itself
prescribes. Concretely, the recommended split is:

a) **B22d-grade.go** (~10 LoC): switch the single `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
   call in cli/internal/agent/grade.go to
   `r.runInternal(RunArgs{...})` via the existing dispatch seam from
   ddx-dd640c18. Satisfies AC items 1, 4, 5 (RunOptions/CompareOptions/
   QuorumOptions removed from grade.go — items 4 and 5 are vacuous as the
   types aren't present today). Build and tests green; runtime-lint green
   because the staged file contains no legacy literals after the swap.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean, AC-checkable.

b) **B22d-cmd-RunViaService** (~30 LoC): switch
   cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462's RunViaService call to
   RunWithConfigViaService, threading a ResolvedConfig through the call.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean.

c) **B22d-cmd-RunBenchmarkViaService** (~30 LoC): same migration for
   :1243 RunBenchmarkViaService → a new RunBenchmarkWithConfigViaService
   that takes ResolvedConfig.

d) **B22d-execute_bead-dispatch** (~80 LoC + 2-3 test files):
   migrate execute_bead.go dispatchAgentRun (line 647 RunOptions
   composite, line 938 RunViaServiceWith) and execute_bead_review.go
   dispatchReviewRun (line 531 RunOptions composite, line 704
   RunViaServiceWith) onto a new internal seam that takes
   ResolvedConfig + AgentRunRuntime. Update the corresponding
   execute_bead_*_test.go files in this bead (they already import the
   functions and stage as siblings).

e) **B22d-BuildArgs-signature** (~150 LoC, mostly tests):
   change BuildArgs's parameter from RunOptions to a new internal
   buildArgsInput struct (or a slice of explicit args). Update
   agent_test.go's 15+ call sites in the same commit. Adjacent runner.go
   call sites stay on RunOptions internally — runner.go is not in the AC
   removal list.

f) **B22d-SessionIndexEntryFromResult-signature** (~80 LoC):
   change SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter to take
   the small subset of fields it actually uses (Harness, Model, Provider,
   Correlation, PromptSource, etc.) as discrete arguments or a small
   internal struct. Update session_index_test.go and the four
   service-run callers in the same commit.

g) **B22d-RunFunc-and-compare-internals** (~250 LoC):
   change RunFunc's signature, runCompareArmWith, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
   the public RunCompareWith/RunQuorumWith/RunBenchmarkWith signatures
   and BenchmarkArmsToCompare's return shape. Update prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
   models_test.go, claude_stream_test.go in the same commit. After this
   step, compare_adapter.go has no RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
   remaining (AC items 2, 3 satisfied).

h) **B22d-service_run-public-API** (~150 LoC):
   delete RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner and
   inline their bodies into the new RunWithConfigViaService internal helper.
   At this point all callers have already moved (steps b, c, d) so the
   deletion is mechanical. service_run.go has no RunOptions remaining
   (AC item 2 satisfied).

After all eight steps, the legacy types are unreachable from any caller,
runner.go's internal RunOptions usage can be retired in B22 final, and
the type definitions in types.go:36/81/171 can be deleted as planned.

The grade.go change in isolation (step a) is genuinely a 10-LoC win and
satisfies 3 of the 7 AC items (1, 4, 5) — but the bead's stated rule
"Do not commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will
accept it" forbids landing it without also satisfying items 2, 3, 6, 7.
Per attempt-1's findings, the only path that satisfies *all* AC items in
*one commit* is the ~14,000 LoC change above, which both exceeds the
SD-024 size budget and is unworkable in a single execute-bead worker
window.

The right shape for this work is the eight-bead split above. Each step
is independently <500 LoC, satisfies SD-024 §Migration discipline, and
ends with a green tree, a passing build, and no runtime-lint violations
on its staged files.

      routes each legacy row through SessionIndexEntryFromLegacy, which
      calls billingModeFor when the row has no stored mode.

      `cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go` asserts the three
      modes land on a mixed fixture and fails if any row is written
      without one.

    - Schema: `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql` lines
      707–717 (type) and 1977–1984 (query).
    - Resolver: `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent.go`
      delegates to ServerState.GetSessionsCostSummaryGraphQL in
      `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:676`, which reads
      month-shard-scoped session index entries and buckets by
      billingMode.
    - Tests: TestSessionsCostSummaryBucketsAndEmptyWindow and
      TestSessionsCostSummaryLocalEstimateUsesConfiguredRate in
      `cli/internal/server/session_cost_summary_test.go` cover the
      three buckets, the empty-window zeroing, and the configured
      local estimate path.

 5. UI — three cards (Cash paid / Subscription value / Local sessions).
    - `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte`
      lines 215–295.

      lines 164–181 assert each card is present and the explanatory
      tooltip appears on hover.

 6. UI — per-row badge.
    - +page.svelte lines 376–392 render a `cash` / `sub` / `local`
      badge with colour classes and an aria-labelled tooltip.
    - Playwright: sessions.spec.ts lines 169, 170, 183–184, 197
      assert each badge class on a seeded fixture with one session
      of each billing mode.

 7. Zero-state.
    - +page.svelte:fmtCardCost returns `—` when no sessions of that
      bucket exist; fmtLocalValue returns `0` when local count is
      zero.
    - Playwright: sessions.spec.ts lines 241–243 assert `—`, `—`,
      and `0` for the three cards on an empty fixture.

 8. Optional local cost config.
    - Schema: `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json` declares
      `cost.local_per_1k_tokens`.
    - Types: `cli/internal/config/types.go` surfaces Cost.LocalPer1KTokens.
    - Resolver: state_graphql.go:localCostPer1KTokens reads it and
      GetSessionsCostSummaryGraphQL multiplies sum(totalTokens) by
      the rate, dividing by 1000.
    - Tests: TestSessionsCostSummaryLocalEstimateUsesConfiguredRate
      covers the set and unset paths; Playwright spec at
      sessions.spec.ts line 286 asserts `$0.42 est.` is rendered.

      independent of the tooltip.

      unified endpoints usage/quota work should reuse those
      billingMode semantics…". `ddx bead show ddx-23978824 --json`

.ddx/attachments/ddx-7aa810b8/events.jsonl:16:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-7aa810b8 — Track execute-bead worktree lifecycle

Verdict: no_changes. The bead is multi-surface and the AC set cannot be
completed to the specified quality in a single pass without committing
partial or red code across several surfaces. Per the bead instructions
("a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit"), this attempt
returns for breakdown/re-queue with a stronger budget or a pre-split bead set.

What the AC demands (11 items, summarized):

  1. GraphQL resolver executeBeadWorktrees(projectId) returning
     {path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state,
     createdAt, lastActivityAt} with unit tests for all four states.
  2. Pure, table-driven state classifier
     (active | completed | abandoned | orphan) with ≥2 edge cases per state.
  3. New SvelteKit route /nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/worktrees — sortable,
     filterable table.
  4. Row actions (Remove, Force-remove) wired through GraphQL mutations
     with confirm-gating and typed confirmation for force-remove.
  5. CLI: `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run] [--project] [--yes]`
     with an integration test against a fixture repo containing one of
     each state.
  6. Server-side sweep with configurable interval (default 15m), a
     GraphQL counter rollup type, and opt-in `worktrees.auto_prune` config.
  7. Cross-links to /executions/<id> (depends on ddx-726bc5f2) and a
     reciprocal "Worktree" section on the execution detail page.
  8. Fixture seed with ≥100 worktrees across states; p95 ≤ 200ms.
  9. Playwright e2e that seeds one of each state, asserts state badges,
     exercises Remove confirm flow, and asserts active-worktree gating.
 10. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ for Part 4 canonical path
     convention + migration plan (required only if Part 4 ships).
 11. Cross-reference notes to ddx-12cae4dd, ddx-71fbe968, ddx-726bc5f2,
     ddx-15f7ee0b.

What is in place already (checked):

  - Server package cli/internal/server/ with graphql resolver module,
    state.go / state_executions.go, workers.go, and a land_coordinator
    — the sweep / state-classification pattern has prior art here.
  - Frontend SvelteKit tree includes /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]
    with executions, workers, beads, documents, etc. No worktrees route yet.
  - Executions detail page already exists at .../executions/[executionId].
    Cross-link target in AC 7 is therefore implementable, not blocked on
    ddx-726bc5f2 (which may itself be partial or separate).
  - Two worktree path conventions confirmed at runtime:
      .claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>/   (branch-based, older)
      /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/ (detached)
    This execution itself is running from the /tmp convention.

What is blocking a single-pass completion:

  - The surface area spans Go (resolver + mutations + sweep + classifier +
    CLI subcommand + config plumbing), SvelteKit (new route, table with
    sort/filter, two confirm dialogs, mutation wiring, Houdini type
    regeneration), fixtures (≥100-worktree seeder), unit tests, an
    integration test, and a Playwright e2e. Each surface has its own
    test-green bar; shipping them all together green within one pass
    risks red commits on the surfaces that get squeezed last.
  - AC 5 (CLI subcommand) requires a new `ddx server state` command
    group; the current CLI surface exposes `ddx server` but does not
    have a `state` subcommand tree — adding one is a small but
    independent surface that interacts with the wider command layout.
  - AC 6 (sweep + counter + config) needs config-schema changes
    (.ddx/config.yaml worktrees block), which touches the config loader
    and every test that loads config. This is a cross-cutting change
    and deserves its own attempt slot.
  - AC 8's ≥100-worktree fixture is cheap to generate but the p95
    claim must be measured against a real handler — per the user's
    standing perf stance (feedback_testing_and_performance), a perf
    claim requires baseline + boundary + harness, not just a seed and
    a one-shot timing.

Recommended breakdown (for re-queue):

  A. Inventory resolver + pure classifier + unit tests.
     (AC 1, AC 2; pure, no UI, no config changes.)
  B. Worktrees route + list view + cross-links to executions.
     (AC 3, AC 7; depends on A; no mutations yet.)
  C. Remove / Force-remove mutations + row actions + Playwright e2e.
     (AC 4, AC 9; depends on B.)
  D. CLI worktree-prune subcommand + integration test.
     (AC 5; can run parallel to C once A is done.)
  E. Sweep + counter + config + opt-in auto_prune.
     (AC 6; depends on A; config-surface change.)
  F. ≥100-worktree fixture + perf harness with p95 measurement.
     (AC 8; depends on A; separate because of the perf methodology bar.)
  G. Path-convention design note + Part 4 migration plan.
     (AC 10, AC 11 for cross-refs; docs-only.)

A follow-up attempt should take A alone, or A+B together, to stay
within a single pass's green-bar budget.

No files were modified in this worktree.
